title: Compare Product Reviews
summary: Read and compare online reviews to judge whether a product is right for you.
learnerPromise: You will be able to read multiple reviews and compare them to make a more informed buying decision.
whyItMatters: Reviews often show real experiences that product descriptions leave out. Learning to compare them helps you avoid disappointment and pick items that match your needs.
realLifeUses: Choosing between two phone cases by reading what buyers say about durability
Deciding if a pair of headphones fits comfortably by checking comfort-related reviews
Checking if a kitchen gadget actually works as advertised before ordering
Comparing recent and older reviews to see if product quality has changed over time
materialsNeeded: A product you are considering buying
result: A short list of pros and cons based on actual buyer reviews.
youWillLearn: How to sort reviews by most recent and most helpful
How to spot repeated themes across multiple reviews
How to weigh positive and negative feedback fairly
How to match review details to your own needs and situation
miniChallenge: Pick one item you might buy and read at least 5 reviews. Write down 2 things buyers liked and 2 things buyers did not like.
steps: Find the product page and locate the reviews or ratings section.
Sort reviews by 'most helpful' or 'most recent' to get a balanced view.
Read at least 5 reviews, including both high and low star ratings.
Look for patterns: what do multiple reviewers mention as good or bad?
Write down 2 pros and 2 cons that matter most to your own situation.
completionCriteria: You have read reviews from multiple star levels
You have written a short pros and cons list based on reviewer feedback
Your list connects review findings to your own needs
commonProblems: Only reading 5-star reviews and missing common complaints
Ignoring the date of reviews and reading outdated feedback
Getting overwhelmed by too many reviews — start with a small number
tips: Look for reviews that mention specific details rather than vague praise
Check the review date — older reviews may reflect an older product version
Pay attention to reviews from people who describe a use case similar to yours
